product_overview: Desktop application that simulates a screw as a damped harmonic oscillator, provides a mechanical-advantage (VM) calculator, and allows simple encrypt/decrypt operations using the screw VM as a key.
core_goals: Explore the dynamic behavior of a screw modeled as a damped harmonic oscillator by adjusting physical parameters and watching an animation and y(t) plot.
Compute the mechanical advantage (VM) from geometric inputs (radius and pitch) quickly and accurately.
Encrypt and decrypt text using the computed VM (and additional parameters) as the key input for the cryptography module.

features.name: Physics Simulation
features.description: Simulate and visualize the screw modeled as a damped harmonic oscillator (RK4 integration), view animation and y(t) graph, and inspect derived indicators (natural frequency, damping ratio, damping type).
user_flows: Navigate to /simulation -> Adjust mass slider -> Adjust damping slider -> Adjust spring constant slider -> Start simulation -> Watch animation -> Observe y(t) graph -> Observe computed natural frequency, damping ratio, and damping type
Navigate to /simulation -> Adjust mass slider -> Start simulation -> Reset simulation -> Observe simulation stopped and parameters reset to defaults

features.name: VM Calculator
features.description: Compute the mechanical advantage (VM) of a screw from radius and pitch inputs and display the numeric result.
user_flows: Navigate to /calculator -> Enter radio value -> Enter pitch value -> Submit VM calculation -> See VM result
Navigate to /calculator -> Enter non-numeric or empty radio or pitch -> Submit VM calculation -> See validation error indicating numeric input is required

features.name: Cryptography
features.description: Encrypt and decrypt text using the screw's VM (derived from radius and pitch) and user-selected number of turns as key material for the crypto operations.
user_flows: Navigate to /crypto -> Enter radio value -> Enter pitch value -> Select number of turns -> Enter plaintext to encrypt -> Submit encryption -> See ciphertext output
Navigate to /crypto -> Enter radio value -> Enter pitch value -> Select number of turns -> Enter ciphertext to decrypt -> Submit decryption -> See plaintext output
Navigate to /crypto -> Enter radio value -> Enter pitch value -> Select number of turns -> Leave text field empty -> Submit encryption -> See validation error indicating text is required

features.name: Physics Simulation
features.description: Simulate screw as damped harmonic oscillator using RK4

features.name: Cryptography
features.description: Encrypt/decrypt data using screw VM as key
